input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-05T01:00:29.697Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to declare values for `Light` and `Dark` inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework watches `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` and swaps values automatically when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme<T>()` or `SetAppThemeColor()`:

```csharp
// For Color properties (convenience helper)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For any typed property
myPage.SetAppTheme<Color>(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Colors.White,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

// Works for non-color properties too
myButton.SetAppTheme<string>(Button.TextProperty,
    light: "Light Mode",
    dark:  "Dark Mode");
```

---

**Key points:**

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Reacts to OS theme changes in real time |
| **Scope** | Works on any `BindableProperty` — colors, strings, images, etc. |
| **Resources** | You can also use it inside `ResourceDictionary` styles for reuse |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme` to force `Light`/`Dark`/`Unspecified` |

For larger apps, define themed styles in `App.xaml` resources so you don't repeat bindings on every element:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Style TargetType="Label">
    <Setter Property="TextColor"
            Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />
</Style>
```
